items.title: Water price spike has farmers fearing for future of irrigation

children.content: WaterNSW in a "death spiral" and irrigators say steep price rises for water will not save it.

image.altText: Corn crop and irrigation infrastructure at first light, near Bathurst

items.title: Juice company fined $250k over forklift crash that took woman's toes

children.content: The Industrial Relations Commission hears the incident at the Real Juice Company was the third involving a forklift at the site, where drivers had received no training.

image.altText: Rows of juice bottles stand in a supermarket.

items.title: 'Glint in their eyes': How Aussies watched the Socceroos match against Paraguay

children.content: Crowds across the nation adorned in green and gold gathered in support of the Socceroos for their third match in the FIFA World Cup.

gallery.altText: A group of young school children sit and watch the game while holding supportive posters.
A large crowd gathered with a smoky red haze coming from the front after a flare was lit.
Two students, one wearing a gold curly wig and one wearing a bright green bob wig hold an Aussie flag.

thumbnail.altText: A group of young school children sit and watch the game while holding supportive posters.
A large crowd gathered with a smoky red haze coming from the front after a flare was lit.
Two students, one wearing a gold curly wig and one wearing a bright green bob wig hold an Aussie flag.

items.title: Two men charged as NSW Police seize 64 kilograms of  drugs

children.content: Police have charged two men and seized almost 64 kilograms of drugs with an estimated street value of more than $2.5 million, north of Newcastle.

image.altText: Police, one face blurred, escort handcuffed man, also blurred, towards police van.

items.title: Daughter arrested after mother's body found at Sydney aged care home

children.content: Police question a 53-year-old woman in Sydney's west about an allegedly administered substance after her mother was found dead in an aged care home.

image.altText: Darrin wears a police hat and uniform and speaks into media microphones.

items.title: Police watchdog orders misconduct investigation after gay club drug raids

children.content: Sydney MP Alex Greenwich said the investigation call by the Enforcement Conduct Commission was a "welcome first step".

gallery.altText: Oxford Street signs and neon motifs in Sydney.
Oxford Street signs and neon motifs in Sydney.
Alex Greenwich speaks into a microphone while wearing a white shirt and dark tie

thumbnail.altText: Oxford Street signs and neon motifs in Sydney.
Oxford Street signs and neon motifs in Sydney.

items.title: Stefanovic thanks audience for 21 years on Today after 'tough' few days

children.content: Today show host Karl Stefanovic will leave Channel Nine after a controversial podcast interview with British far-right figure Tommy Robinson.

image.altText: Karl wears a suit and sits at a desk in a television studio. A smiling woman sits next to him.

items.title: Police shoot and critically injure unarmed man charging at officers

children.content: A man has undergone emergency surgery after he was shot by police in Western Sydney.

image.altText: A police van parked on a street surrounded by police tape.

items.title: Man who tried to 'win way out of this life' calls for gambling changes

children.content: A Sydney man who spent more than $160,000 on lottery tickets and poker machines in less than two years says his experience shows "more needs to be done" to protect vulnerable community members.

gallery.altText: A man in a black shirt sits on a wooden bench looking away from the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a black shirt sits on a wooden bench looking away from the camera.

items.title: New bridge announced but Great Western Highway closed for another year

children.content: A new bridge will be built at Mitchell's Causeway on the Great Western Highway to link Sydney and the state's Central West.

image.altText: A 3D render showing pylons  supporting a bridge span

items.title: Stefanovic's move to outsider politics follows a well-worn lucrative path

children.content: Listening to the culture war showman schtick he has so quickly adopted, Karl Stefanovic is now less a newsman than an ideological performer.

image.altText: Two men in black on a street.

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: Abalone divers say 41 per cent catch reduction 'a kick in the face'

children.content: The NSW government says a reset is needed to ensure the sustainable future of the abalone fishery along the south coast, but commercial divers say the reduction is a "pretty disgusting state of affairs".

gallery.altText: a man with a scruffy beard looks at a camera with a hand on his hip.
Close-up of a small pile of fresh abalone on rocks

thumbnail.altText: a man with a scruffy beard looks at a camera with a hand on his hip.
Close-up of a small pile of fresh abalone on rocks

items.title: Why the Moana live action movie has come so soon

children.content: Dwayne Johnson and Sydneysider Catherine Lagaʻaia have walked the blue carpet at a special event in Sydney. They told ABC News why now is the perfect time for a live action Moana.

image.altText: Catherine Lagaʻaia and Dwayne Johnson standing in front of a Moana sign with flowers and plants surrounding them

items.title: Warning fire ants could kill thousands of pets a year

children.content: If Australia does not ramp up fire ant eradication efforts, the invasive species will cause billion-dollar economic impacts and dozens of deaths, a study finds.

items.title: 'Overjoyed': Shark attack victim reunited with daughter after waking from coma

children.content: The 34-year-old's brother said she was awake, speaking with loved ones and her medical team and "remembers the whole event in detail".

image.altText: Sydney mother and Coogee local, Leah Stewart, who was mauled by a shark holds a baby and looks at the camera

items.title: ICAC told of request to alter university contract timelines

children.content: The corruption watchdog has been told a University of Wollongong manager was asked to amend timelines to obscure a potential conflict of interest involving a senior staffer and a consultancy contract that would eventually be worth $3.7 million.

items.title: Swimming group takes 'chilling with friends' to another level

children.content: Young people are turning to local community groups to get out of their comfort zones, make real connections and boost their wellbeing.

gallery.altText: A group of people swim in an ocean pool at sunrise.

thumbnail.altText: A group of people swim in an ocean pool at sunrise.

items.title: How Karl Stefanovic went from Today show glory to 'Joe Bogan'

children.content: Today show host Karl Stefanovic is reportedly leaving Channel Nine after a more than 20-year career at the network that was filled with highs and lows.

image.altText: Mr Stefanovic wearing a black blazer and bow tie with a white dress shirt standing and smiling.

items.title: Illawarra man admits to making offensive online comment about PM

children.content: A Wollongong man has pleaded guilty to using a carriage service to menace, harass, or cause offence after posting an online comment about Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese.

image.altText: Man with dark long hair and facial hair, denim button-up shirt outside brick court building

items.title: The alternative burial method that could soon come to Australia

children.content: Advocates say human composting offers a sustainable, greener alternative to burials and crematoriums, and can also provide a cheaper and equitable option after death.

gallery.altText: A human compost vessel at recompose facility in Seattle.

thumbnail.altText: A human compost vessel at recompose facility in Seattle.

items.title: New metro line promise and Eminem feature in NSW budget reply

children.content: New South Wales opposition leader Kellie Sloane has channelled the American rapper Eminem in a budget reply that promised businesses a tax cut and Sydneysiders a new metro line.

image.altText: Kellie Sloane NSW Liberal Leader talks to the media at announcement of mick willing as their candiate for camden for 2027 vote

items.title: 72yo man accused of 'offensive' graffiti banned from Sydney suburbs

children.content: A 72-year-old man arrested over dozens of alleged graffiti and malicious damage offences in Sydney's inner west has been granted bail, after a judge expressed surprise that he was not charged under NSW hate speech laws.

image.altText: An older man with short grey hair wearing a black undershirt and button-up chequered longsleeve walks on a path.

items.title: 'Heartbreaking': Sydney boarding house to be turned into luxury flats

children.content: A rundown boarding house which was once a haven for vulnerable people in inner Sydney is set to be converted into luxury apartments.

gallery.altText: Beige two-storey apartment building in dim, flat lighting

thumbnail.altText: Beige two-storey apartment building in dim, flat lighting
